experiences.intentions: The board is empty aside from the active L near the top in its spawn orientation. With an empty field, the priority is efficient placement for future line clears while preserving flexibility for upcoming S, O, I, J pieces. Candidate moves: left/right only shift; rotations change footprint but are not urgently needed on an empty board; soft_drop advances slowly. Hard dropping now is strongest because there are no obstacles, and the current horizontal L can settle at the bottom to start a clean stack while moving the game forward immediately.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is almost empty except for an L fixed at the bottom center-left and the active S near the top. Goal is to place the S to keep the stack flat and set up for upcoming O and I pieces. Since there are no immediate threats and horizontal movement can be done before locking, the best immediate sub-goal is to drop the S efficiently toward the bottom. A hard drop places it now; on this board the S will land resting on the L structure, creating a relatively compact shape without wasting turns. Left/right or rotation are less urgent than getting the piece down.

experiences.intentions: Active I piece is vertical near the center top with an uneven stack below: O block at columns 4-5 on rows 15-16, then S/L structure creating a shallow notch. No immediate line clear is available from the current high position. Goal is to place the I efficiently, ideally preserving flexibility and avoiding a flat horizontal placement high above the stack. Candidate actions: rotate would make it horizontal too high and less useful; hard/soft drop now would commit the current column placement without exploring better alignment; no_op wastes a move. Shifting right can move the vertical I toward the open right side, where a vertical drop is likely safer and may set up future J/Z/T placements around the existing right-leaning stack. So the best immediate sub-goal is repositioning before dropping.

Surface below has a notable valley around columns 3-4 above the J/I/OO stack, and future queue L,T,Z,I favors preserving flatness. Best sub-goal now is horizontal repositioning left.

Goal is to place the S efficiently without creating awkward overhangs; immediate sub-goal is to move it toward a useful landing zone above the existing S/Z/L contour on the lower right-center. Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit immediately in the current central position, likely too early and not aligned with the best fit; soft_drop also commits downward before repositioning; rotations are not clearly needed yet because the flat S orientation can likely fit later; no_op wastes time. Moving right starts guiding the piece toward the open right-center where an S can pair with the lower SS/SL structure and preserve the left side for upcoming L,T,Z,I pieces.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is an S near the top with plenty of empty space below; the stack is low and uneven, with a small cavity around columns 2-4 and a right-side slope near the existing S/L pieces. Goal is to place the current S efficiently without creating overhangs, and since next pieces are L,T,Z,I we can keep the center-left accessible. Immediate horizontal movement is more useful than dropping now because the spawn area is high and we need to steer toward a better landing zone; rotating to horizontal could be useful later, but first shifting left starts positioning toward the center where S can slot more cleanly than on the far right. Candidate actions: hard_drop commits too early on the right; soft_drop reduces maneuver time; rotate actions change shape but not location yet; right worsens edge placement. Best action now is to move left.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top in vertical orientation, above a relatively low uneven stack. The strongest immediate opportunity is to place it into the left-side notch around rows 17-19: with one counterclockwise rotation to horizontal, the S can likely bridge the gap over the existing SS/L structure and help smooth the surface without creating overhangs. So rotate left now.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is S near the top-middle in horizontal orientation above a mostly uneven stack. The right side has a 2-wide/3-wide surface around columns 5-7, while the left has clutter from T/Z. Goal is to place the S cleanly without creating overhangs and set up for upcoming L/T/Z/I. Immediate movement is needed before dropping; rotating vertical now would better fit future descent into uneven terrain and preserve flatter surfaces. Among candidates, hard/soft drop now would commit from current x and likely leave the S stranded high; left/right without rotation is less flexible. Rotating right prepares a narrower profile and keeps options open next turn.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the upper-left with current orientation vertical-right (rotation 1). The stack is low and there is no immediate danger, so the priority is efficient placement to avoid creating isolated holes. Around the left side there is an existing L/T/Z structure at the bottom; moving this L left can help fill the left wall contour better than dropping immediately in its current column, while rotation is not yet necessary because the current shape can still be repositioned. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place it prematurely high on the left-middle and likely waste flexibility; soft_drop also commits downward before aligning; rotations are optional later but not needed this turn. A single left shift improves future fit options and preserves flexibility.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the upper-left, currently vertical with foot to the right. The stack below has a left-side valley and uneven surface; immediate threats are low since the board is only about half full. Goal now is to place the L to improve the left structure and avoid creating overhangs. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place the current vertical orientation into the far-left area but not optimally; soft_drop commits downward too early. Rotating could make a flatter placement later, but first shifting left positions the piece toward the open left wall where L placements are strongest. Right moves away from the useful gap. Best sub-goal is to move left before dropping/rotating further.

experiences.intentions: The active T is high and centered with lots of vertical space below; immediate threats are low because the stack is only in the bottom quarter. The surface has a notable valley around columns 3-4 and generally lower center-left than right. My goal now is to improve future placement flexibility and avoid wasting the T flat at the top. Candidate moves: hard_drop would commit too early without lateral setup; soft_drop only lowers without improving alignment; rotations alone don't exploit the open field yet; right moves toward a flatter area but less useful than aiming for the center-left notch. Moving left starts positioning the T toward the central gap where it can later drop/rotate efficiently, with minimal risk given the empty airspace.

The stack below is low and uneven, with a noticeable left-side cavity near rows 17-19 and more open space center/right. Rotating right would make a vertical Z that can fit future notches, but there is no urgent need to lock yet and moving horizontally first is safer to reach a better placement. experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I tetromino currently horizontal near the top with a completely open field beneath. Surface below is uneven but there is no immediate threat at the spawn area. Goal now is to place the I efficiently; the strongest immediate sub-goal is to exploit the large empty vertical space and orient the I vertically so it can be steered into a useful column on later turns. Among candidates, rotate_right changes the I from horizontal to vertical while keeping options open; left/right only shift the current horizontal bar, and dropping now would commit to a weak high placement. Therefore rotate_right is best.

The stack below is uneven but there is no immediate threat near the top, so the priority is to place the I efficiently for future line clears and avoid wasting the flat horizontal placement high up. Rotating the I vertical now creates a flexible piece that can be moved into a column well on subsequent turns; dropping horizontally here would likely strand it across empty space. Candidate actions: left/right only shift the current horizontal piece and do not improve its shape yet; soft/hard drop would commit to a poor high placement; no_op does nothing. rotate_right is best to turn the I vertical and set up a better descent path.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active I piece is vertical in column 4 near the top, with open space below until it would land on the stack around row 8. The surface has a useful 4-wide horizontal gap on row 14 at columns 4-7 already filled by an old I, so no immediate tetris well exists; the cleaner short-term goal is to place this I to reduce future unevenness without creating overhangs. Moving right would align the vertical I over column 5, where it can eventually drop into a relatively open channel; rotating horizontal right now would span columns 3-6 high above the stack and is less committal this turn. A soft/hard drop immediately in the current column would stack over column 4 above the Z/S area and worsen the center. Best immediate setup is to shift right toward the cleaner channel.

experiences.intentions: Active J piece is high near the top at columns 3-5. The stack below has a central-left cavity and a relatively flat open area on the far left upper rows, while the right side already has a vertical I and some structure. Immediate threat is low because the piece is near spawn with lots of vertical space. Goal is to place J to improve surface and avoid creating inaccessible holes for upcoming O,Z,T,L. Candidate moves: hard/soft drop now would place the current horizontal J around columns 3-5, but better placement likely needs lateral movement first. Rotating now to vertical could fit later, but there is no urgency and moving left opens the useful left-side well/cavity. Moving right heads toward the more obstructed center-right. Best sub-goal is to start shifting the J left toward the open left-center area.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is high on the left with open space below; the stack is low-to-mid with no immediate danger. Best short-term goal is to place the J efficiently without creating extra holes and to preserve flexibility for upcoming O,Z,T,L. From the spawn area, moving right/left only repositions without committing, rotation can be deferred because current orientation can still be useful, and hard drop is premature without better alignment. Soft dropping advances the piece toward the surface while keeping future placement options open next turn.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is J near the upper-left/center, currently spanning columns roughly 2-5 with open air below. Surface has a notable vertical well around column 3-4 and clutter on the left; immediate threat is low because stack is still far from top. Goal is to place J to improve surface and potentially set up clean placements for upcoming O/Z/T/L. Among actions, moving left shifts the J toward the left-side cavity where J is more useful; moving right sends it over flatter center-right where it may conflict with existing I/O structure. Rotations could help later, but first aligning horizontally toward the left is the strongest setup while there is time. Hard/soft drop now would commit too early.

experiences.intentions: Current active piece is J near the upper-middle left, with open space below and a vertical I occupying column 5 that creates a narrow boundary. The stack is low overall, so the main goal is to place the J cleanly to maintain a flat surface and avoid creating holes. At its current orientation, a hard drop would likely land awkwardly on uneven terrain; rotating can better fit the left-side contours. Among options, rotate_right changes the J into a vertical form with the foot extending left, giving more flexibility to slot it beside existing blocks on subsequent moves while still safe because there is plenty of height. This best advances setup for a cleaner placement.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a vertical I in a narrow central column above a fairly uneven stack. Immediate threat is limited because the piece is still high, so best play is to improve placement before committing. The board has a clear 4-wide horizontal gap on row 14 at columns 4-7 already filled by a previous I, and current surface suggests the center-right column is occupied beneath, so hard/soft dropping now would just lock a vertical I suboptimally. Rotations available keep it vertical, with rotation 3 shifting the footprint slightly left into a better lane above columns 2-4. Goal is to position the I for a more useful future drop, likely toward the left/center to fill wells or set up cleaner stacking. Among actions, rotate_right is the only move that changes column occupancy immediately without committing downward; left/right only translate one cell, while rotate_right reaches the valid shifted orientation shown and opens better follow-up movement.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is the vertical I at column 5, high above a relatively uneven stack. Immediate goal is to place it safely and set up for upcoming Z,T,L,O without creating overhangs. The strongest opportunity is the clear 4-cell-deep vertical well already aligned under the I around column 5; moving sideways or rotating is unnecessary and risks losing the clean fit. Among candidates, soft_drop advances toward that placement while preserving flexibility next turn, whereas hard_drop commits immediately. Since only one action is allowed now, soft_drop is the best incremental move toward the goal.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is an O at the top centered around columns 4-5, rows 2-3. O does not benefit from rotation, so movement/drop matters. The surface below has a vertical I in column 5 near the top-middle and various uneven stacks lower down; directly under the O there is empty space for several rows, so immediate danger is low. My goal is to place the O efficiently to flatten the stack or fill a useful 2x2 cavity. Since O cannot rotate and there is no urgent need to shift laterally before gravity, the best immediate sub-goal is to advance placement downward to gather more board information quickly and preserve flexibility. Among actions, left/right commit to a side without clear immediate payoff, rotations are irrelevant, and hard_drop may lock too early without confirming best column. Soft_drop safely progresses toward landing while keeping future horizontal adjustment options.
